summary: The papers focus on advancing small UAS detection, tracking, and neutralization through a combination of visual tracking for gas measurement applications, high-power microwave simulation frameworks for non-kinetic counter-UAS design, lightweight neural network architectures for real-time small UAV detection, and embedded platform solutions to resolve resolution-speed trade-offs in UAV tracking. One study integrates open-path gas sensing with visual cooperative drone tracking, while another develops a multi-physics simulation framework to evaluate HPM-based counter-UAS performance. A lightweight detection model, YolovN-CBi, is introduced for efficient small UAV identification, and a heterogeneous observer-follower framework is proposed to maintain high-resolution tracking on embedded devices without sacrificing detection accuracy.
